Part: Administration And Archive.

Chapter: Superseded Project Dashboard.
Current document status: Superseded.

Section: Project Dashboard.

The two project dashboards were consolidated to prevent conflicting owners, priorities, and status labels.

Use the Current Work Dashboard as the current dashboard and source of truth.

This redirect page remains only so older links and exported references do not break.

End of chapter: Superseded Project Dashboard.

Chapter: Document Inventory.
Current document status: Working Record.

Section: Document Inventory.

This inventory lists the active draft documents, templates, and imported source materials currently in the repository.

Important: Source materials are reference copies. A source document should not be treated as current policy unless the appropriate committee, leadership body, or congregation confirms it.

Section: Inventory Conventions.

Handbook: Yes means the document should likely become a direct section of the future KBC Financial Handbook.
Handbook: Maybe means the content should inform the manual, or may belong in an appendix, but needs review first.
Handbook: No means the document should remain a source, project-control document, template, communication draft, or archived reference.

Section: Active Draft Documents.

Table summary.
File name: docs/document-inventory.md. Type of document: Inventory / project control. Purpose: Track source and draft documents in the repository.. Current status: Draft. Related committee/body: All project reviewers. Handbook: No. Open questions: Who owns keeping this inventory current?.
File name: docs/project-dashboard.md. Type of document: Superseded dashboard redirect. Purpose: Preserve older links while directing readers to the canonical leader-friendly dashboard.. Current status: Superseded. Related committee/body: Documentation stewards. Handbook: No. Open questions: None; update only if the canonical dashboard path changes..
File name: docs/document-stewardship.md. Type of document: Administrative guide. Purpose: Define document ownership, stewardship, review, approval, lifecycle, and archival responsibilities.. Current status: Needs Leadership Review. Related committee/body: Leadership, committees, documentation stewards, Church Clerk. Handbook: No. Open questions: Who will serve as the first two documentation stewards?.
File name: docs/00-project-brief.md. Type of document: Project brief. Purpose: Explain the Treasurer transition, deeper operations issue, and desired future state.. Current status: Draft. Related committee/body: Finance Committee, Personnel Committee, Pastor, Deacons. Handbook: Maybe. Open questions: Should this remain a project brief or become a short manual introduction?.
File name: docs/01-current-state-assessment.md. Type of document: Assessment. Purpose: Organize current problems by role clarity, committee ownership, manual processes, software, spending, budget, and documentation.. Current status: Draft. Related committee/body: Finance Committee, Personnel Committee, Pastor, Deacons. Handbook: Maybe. Open questions: Which observations should be validated by Finance Committee before being used publicly?.
File name: docs/02-decision-log.md. Type of document: Decision log. Purpose: Record project decisions, owners, status, and notes.. Current status: Draft. Related committee/body: All project reviewers. Handbook: No. Open questions: Who is responsible for updating decisions after meetings?.
File name: docs/03-open-questions.md. Type of document: Project issue register. Purpose: Track unresolved questions for Personnel, Finance, Nominating, Deacons, Pastor, Congregation, and professional review.. Current status: Working Record. Related committee/body: All project reviewers. Handbook: No. Open questions: Which questions must be answered before any church vote?.
File name: docs/implementation-roadmap.md. Type of document: Implementation roadmap. Purpose: Organize stabilization, 30-day, 60-day, 90-day, and long-term Handbook integration work.. Current status: Draft. Related committee/body: Finance Committee, Personnel Committee, Pastor, Deacons, Treasurer, Bookkeeper. Handbook: No. Open questions: Who owns roadmap updates and phase completion tracking?.
File name: docs/document-workflow-upgrade-notes.md. Type of document: Workflow review note. Purpose: Capture useful patterns and upgrade opportunities from the prior KBC Personnel Committee repository.. Current status: Draft. Related committee/body: Personnel Committee, document maintainers. Handbook: No. Open questions: Which workflow improvements should be implemented next, especially Windows-friendly local export?.
File name: docs/governance/constitution-and-bylaws-reference.md. Type of document: Governance reference page. Purpose: Provide site access to the 2018 Constitution, Bylaws, and Covenant source and key financial-operations review questions.. Current status: Reference. Related committee/body: Congregation, Pastor, Deacons, Nominating Committee, Finance Committee, Personnel Committee. Handbook: Maybe. Open questions: Is the 2018 PDF the current authoritative version?.
File name: docs/governance/financial-operations-view.md. Type of document: Governance diagram. Purpose: Provide a durable visual of financial accountability, operations, committee oversight, and congregational authority.. Current status: Needs Bylaw Review. Related committee/body: Congregation, Finance Committee, Personnel Committee, Treasurer, Bookkeeper. Handbook: Yes. Open questions: Does the diagram match the bylaws and adopted reporting relationships?.
File name: docs/governance/finance-committee-charter.md. Type of document: Committee charter. Purpose: Define Finance Committee purpose, responsibilities, meeting cadence, monthly review, software ownership, and reporting.. Current status: Draft. Related committee/body: Finance Committee. Handbook: Yes. Open questions: Does this match KBC bylaws and current committee authority?.
File name: docs/governance/personnel-committee-role.md. Type of document: Committee role clarification. Purpose: Clarify Personnel Committee role in finance-related staffing, job descriptions, accountability, and evaluations.. Current status: Draft. Related committee/body: Personnel Committee. Handbook: Maybe. Open questions: Should this live in a broader personnel manual instead of the financial operations manual?.
File name: docs/governance/responsibility-matrix.md. Type of document: Responsibility matrix. Purpose: Show which committee/body owns which finance operations decisions.. Current status: Draft. Related committee/body: Personnel Committee, Finance Committee, Nominating Committee, Deacons, Pastor, Treasurer, Bookkeeper, Congregation. Handbook: Yes. Open questions: Which cells require bylaw confirmation or church practice confirmation?.
File name: docs/governance/future-governance-expansion.md. Type of document: Parking lot / governance review note. Purpose: Preserve future review questions for Benevolence, other committee spending, Deacon counsel, and congregational approval paths.. Current status: Parking Lot. Related committee/body: Pastor, Deacons, Finance Committee, Personnel Committee, Nominating Committee, Congregation if required. Handbook: Maybe. Open questions: Which future issues should wait until Bookkeeper hiring and immediate financial controls are stable?.
File name: docs/roles/treasurer-governance-role.md. Type of document: Role description. Purpose: Draft the elected Treasurer / Financial Officer role focused on governance, accountability, reporting, and Finance Committee coordination.. Current status: Draft. Related committee/body: Treasurer, Finance Committee, Nominating Committee, Congregation. Handbook: Yes. Open questions: Should day-to-day bookkeeping be optional, interim-only, or removed from this role?.
File name: docs/roles/bookkeeper-financial-administrator-job-description.md. Type of document: Job description. Purpose: Draft operational paid Bookkeeper / Financial Administrator duties, qualifications, software responsibilities, and accountability.. Current status: Draft. Related committee/body: Personnel Committee, Finance Committee. Handbook: Maybe. Open questions: What compensation, hours, classification, supervision, and Finance Committee duty/control input are needed before hiring?.
File name: docs/roles/bookkeeper-hiring-process.md. Type of document: Hiring process / checklist. Purpose: Provide hiring checklist, interview guide, reference questions, and candidate evaluation rubric for the authorized paid Bookkeeper role.. Current status: Draft. Related committee/body: Personnel Committee, Finance Committee. Handbook: No. Open questions: Who will serve as hiring lead, interview coordinator, reference checker, and Finance workflow reviewer?.
File name: docs/roles/bookkeeper-job-application.md. Type of document: Job application. Purpose: Provide a draft application for a Bookkeeper / Financial Administrator in the style of existing KBC forms.. Current status: Draft. Related committee/body: Personnel Committee. Handbook: No. Open questions: What final application language and background-check requirements are needed?.
File name: docs/roles/interim-treasurer-role.md. Type of document: Interim role description. Purpose: Define temporary coverage expectations while Treasurer role is vacant or transitioning.. Current status: Draft. Related committee/body: Finance Committee, Nominating Committee, Deacons, Congregation. Handbook: Maybe. Open questions: What is the term, authority, and reporting path for the interim Treasurer?.
File name: docs/roles/treasurer-vs-bookkeeper-duty-split.md. Type of document: Duty split analysis. Purpose: Compare current Treasurer duties with proposed Treasurer, Bookkeeper, committee, and outside vendor responsibilities.. Current status: Draft. Related committee/body: Finance Committee, Personnel Committee, Treasurer, Bookkeeper, Nominating Committee. Handbook: Maybe. Open questions: Which duty split changes require bylaw review, committee approval, or church vote?.
File name: docs/policies/spending-authority-policy.md. Type of document: Policy. Purpose: Define spending categories, approval paths, and placeholder thresholds.. Current status: Draft. Related committee/body: Finance Committee, Congregation if vote required. Handbook: Yes. Open questions: What dollar thresholds and church vote triggers apply?.
File name: docs/policies/financial-authority-policy-package.md. Type of document: Policy review package. Purpose: Coordinate spending, reimbursement, payment-card, exception, and congregational approval decisions.. Current status: Needs Finance Review. Related committee/body: Finance Committee, Treasurer, Congregation if required. Handbook: No. Open questions: Which thresholds and approval paths should Finance recommend?.
File name: docs/policies/reimbursement-policy.md. Type of document: Policy. Purpose: Define reimbursement documentation, routine expense treatment, pre-approval needs, and review process.. Current status: Draft. Related committee/body: Finance Committee. Handbook: Yes. Open questions: Who approves routine reimbursements, and what submission deadline applies?.
File name: docs/policies/credit-card-policy.md. Type of document: Policy. Purpose: Define controlled payment/card use, cardholder responsibilities, receipt rules, monthly review, and revocation.. Current status: Draft. Related committee/body: Finance Committee. Handbook: Yes. Open questions: What card/payment method should KBC use and who may hold cards?.
File name: docs/policies/offering-counting-and-deposit-policy.md. Type of document: Policy. Purpose: Define controls for counting, recording, and depositing offerings.. Current status: Draft. Related committee/body: Finance Committee, Treasurer, Bookkeeper. Handbook: Yes. Open questions: What current counting process is used and what controls are required?.
File name: docs/policies/monthly-financial-review-policy.md. Type of document: Policy. Purpose: Define monthly review items for reports, reconciliations, budget status, and exceptions.. Current status: Draft. Related committee/body: Finance Committee, Treasurer. Handbook: Yes. Open questions: What exact monthly packet should be required?.
File name: docs/policies/audit-and-review-policy.md. Type of document: Policy. Purpose: Define periodic audit, review, or external accounting expectations.. Current status: Draft. Related committee/body: Finance Committee, Congregation if vote required. Handbook: Yes. Open questions: What level of review is appropriate for KBC and who performs it?.
File name: docs/procedures/weekly-bookkeeping-checklist.md. Type of document: Procedure / checklist. Purpose: Provide repeatable weekly bookkeeping steps.. Current status: Draft. Related committee/body: Bookkeeper, Treasurer, Finance Committee. Handbook: Yes. Open questions: What exact weekly tasks are performed today?.
File name: docs/procedures/monthly-close-checklist.md. Type of document: Procedure / checklist. Purpose: Provide repeatable month-end close steps and report preparation.. Current status: Draft. Related committee/body: Bookkeeper, Treasurer, Finance Committee. Handbook: Yes. Open questions: What system reports and reconciliations are required each month?.

Purpose: Provide recurring monthly Finance Committee meeting checklist for income/expenses, budget, designated funds, cards, reimbursements, exceptions, votes, and business meeting reporting.. Current status: Draft. Related committee/body: Finance Committee, Treasurer, Bookkeeper. Handbook: Yes. Open questions: Who prepares the packet, records decisions, and maintains the checklist?.
File name: docs/procedures/payroll-process.md. Type of document: Procedure. Purpose: Document payroll inputs, approval flow, provider/software steps, and records.. Current status: Draft. Related committee/body: Personnel Committee, Finance Committee, Treasurer, Bookkeeper. Handbook: Yes. Open questions: Who owns payroll changes, approvals, and professional review?.

Current status: Draft. Related committee/body: Finance Committee, Treasurer, Bookkeeper. Handbook: Yes. Open questions: Who enters contributions today, and how is donor confidentiality protected?.

Current status: Draft. Related committee/body: Pastor, Deacons, Finance Committee, Personnel Committee, Congregation. End of chapter: Document Inventory.

Chapter: Export Process.
Current document status: Current.

Section: Export and Release Process.

This page explains how to turn selected Markdown artifacts into review files and release bundles.

Section: What To Edit.

Edit the Markdown source files in dist/:

dist/leadership-review-packet.md.
dist/one-page-congregational-summary.md.
dist/congregational-slide-deck.md.

The Bookkeeper job description export is generated from:

docs/roles/bookkeeper-financial-administrator-job-description.md.

Do not edit generated PDF, DOCX, or PPTX files as the long-term source. Regenerate them from Markdown instead.

The leadership task tracker is also in dist/:

dist/leadership-review-task-tracker.csv.

Open it in Excel or Google Sheets to update task owners, due dates, status, and notes after leadership or committee discussion.

Section: Export Review Files.

Run:

Command or example: make export.

This creates PDF, DOCX, and PPTX review files in dist/exports/ and then validates them.

Section: Validate Existing Exports.

Run:

Command or example: make validate.

Validation checks that the expected files exist, are not zero bytes, have readable file structure, meet page-count expectations, keep the congregational handout to one page, keep the leadership packet to 12 pages or fewer, keep internal metadata out of the public handout, and scan PDFs for obvious artifact characters.

Section: Clean Generated Files.

Run:

Command or example: make clean.

This removes generated files from dist/exports/. It does not remove dated release bundles.

Section: Create a Release Packet.

Run:

Command or example: make release.

This runs export and validation, creates a dated folder under dist/releases/YYYY-MM-DD/, copies the generated files and task tracker into that folder, creates dist/releases/YYYY-MM-DD.zip, and prints the release paths.

Section: Create a Listening Script.

Run:

Command or example: make audiobook.

This creates a comprehensive compiled Markdown document and a cleaner text-to-speech script in dist/audiobook/.

The TTS script is meant for listening, not formal approval. Review it before uploading it to a text-to-speech app.

The same command also creates smaller text chunks in dist/audiobook/chunks/ and an index at dist/audiobook/chunk-index.csv.

Section: Create Local Audio With Piper.

Install Piper:

Command or example: python -m pip install piper-tts.

Download the default voice:

Command or example: make tts-local-download-voice.

Generate one sample WAV file:

Command or example: make tts-local-sample.

Generate all local WAV files:

Command or example: make tts-local.

The full Piper run may take a while because the audiobook is split into many chunks. Use the sample command first.

If Piper stops partway through with a WAV write error, rerun the same command. The script skips valid WAV files, regenerates incomplete WAV files, and resumes from the remaining chunks.

Generated audio is placed in dist/audiobook/audio/. Local Piper voice models are placed in dist/audiobook/piper-voices/. Both folders are ignored by Git.

Section: Review Status Labels.

Use clear review status language in document titles, notes, or release communication:

Draft.
Needs Committee Review.
Needs Finance Review.
Needs Personnel Review.
Needs Bylaw Review.
Needs Professional Review.
Ready for Church Consideration.
Approved.
Superseded.

If local export tools are not installed, use the GitHub Action named Export Docs to create downloadable export and release artifacts.

Section: Public Content Audit.

Run:

Command or example: make audit-public.

This checks tracked text files for obvious private-data or secret patterns and warns that binary files under source-materials/ need manual review before broad public sharing.

Section: Sensitive Information Warning.

Do not store donor records, payroll details, bank account numbers, Social Security numbers, passwords, candidate applications, background-check results, or confidential personnel information in this repository or in generated release packets.

End of chapter: Export Process.

Chapter: Exported Files and Release Bundles.
Current document status: Current.

Section: Exported Files and Release Bundles.

The handbook site is for browsing current guidance, current work, and safe historical records. Exports and releases are for formal review packets.

Section: Main Difference.

Use the handbook site when leaders need to read, search, or review material.
Use exports when leaders need PDF, DOCX, PPTX, or spreadsheet files for a meeting packet.
Use releases when the church needs a dated bundle of review files.
Use the NotebookLM sourcebook when leaders need one Markdown source that can be uploaded to NotebookLM for question-and-answer review across the current materials.
Use the audiobook/TTS bundle when leaders want a listening version of the documentation.

Section: Source Files.

The main release source files live in dist/:

dist/leadership-review-packet.md.
dist/one-page-congregational-summary.md.
dist/congregational-slide-deck.md.
dist/leadership-review-task-tracker.csv.

The NotebookLM sourcebook is generated at:

dist/notebooklm/kbc-financial-operations-sourcebook.md.
dist/notebooklm/kbc-financial-operations-sourcebook.pdf if the local PDF tools are available.

The audiobook/TTS bundle is generated at:

dist/audiobook/kbc-financial-operations-complete-document.md.
dist/audiobook/kbc-financial-operations-tts-script.txt.
dist/audiobook/chunks/.
dist/audiobook/chunk-index.csv.

The Bookkeeper job description export is generated from:

docs/roles/bookkeeper-financial-administrator-job-description.md.

Section: Generated Files.

Generated review files are placed in dist/exports/.

Dated release bundles are placed in dist/releases/YYYY-MM-DD/ and zipped as dist/releases/YYYY-MM-DD.zip.

Generated files are not the long-term source of truth. If a PDF, DOCX, PPTX, or spreadsheet needs a content change, update the Markdown or CSV source first and regenerate the exports.

Section: Commands.

Run:

Command or example: make export.

to create review files and validate them.

Run:

Command or example: make release.

to create a dated release bundle.

Run:

Command or example: make notebooklm.

to create the NotebookLM sourcebook.

Run:

Command or example: make audiobook.

to create a comprehensive compiled Markdown document and a cleaner text-to-speech script.

Run:

Command or example: make tts-local-sample.

to create one sample WAV file with Piper after Piper and a voice model have been installed.

Run:

Command or example: make tts-local.

to generate local Piper WAV files for the full audiobook.

Section: GitHub Pages Warning.

The MkDocs site is published through GitHub Pages when the manual deploy workflow is run.

Treat the live site and public repository as public unless settings are intentionally changed and verified. Review content before deploying updates.

Run make audit-public before broad public sharing or major publishing updates.

End of chapter: Exported Files and Release Bundles.

Chapter: Document Workflow Notes.
Current document status: Working Record.

Section: Document Workflow Upgrade Notes.

Section: Purpose.

This note captures lessons from the prior Kingsville Baptist Church Personnel Committee document repository that should inform this financial operations repository.

The goal is to keep what worked, avoid repeating fragile patterns, and build a cleaner workflow for drafting, review, approval, export, and release.

Section: Source Areas Reviewed.

Kingsville Baptist Church/Personnel Committee/0_Admin & Reference/.
Kingsville Baptist Church/Personnel Committee/1_Job Descriptions/.
Kingsville Baptist Church/Personnel Committee/2_Evaluation & Accountability Frameworks/.
Kingsville Baptist Church/Personnel Committee/4_Committee Working Docs/Custodian Hiring 2026/.
Kingsville Baptist Church/Personnel Committee/5_Final Approved Documents (PDF only)/.
Kingsville Baptist Church/Personnel Committee/convert_gdocs.py.
Kingsville Baptist Church/Personnel Committee/GDOC_CONVERSION_CHECKLIST.md.

Section: Patterns Worth Keeping.

Section: Standard Job Description Structure.

The existing KBC job descriptions generally use this pattern:

Kingsville Baptist Church.
Role title.
Revision date.
Position Summary.
Core Responsibilities.
Accountability and Support.
Character / lifestyle / work ethic expectations.
Compensation and schedule, where applicable.
Terms of Service.

The Bookkeeper / Financial Administrator job description has been reshaped to follow this pattern more closely.

Section: Clear Draft And Final Separation.

The older repository separated working drafts from final approved PDFs. That is helpful and should continue in a modernized way:

Markdown files should be the editable source of truth.
Generated PDF, DOCX, and PPTX files should be review or release copies.
Approved documents should be clearly marked as approved and bundled in dated releases.

Section: KBC Naming Convention.

The older naming guide used:

KBC_[Document_Title]Rev[MonthYear].

This is still useful for final approved documents and formal release files. Working source files can remain shorter and easier to edit in Markdown.

Section: Hiring Process Toolkit.

The Custodian hiring materials provide a good pattern for future hiring work:

Hiring checklist.
Candidate evaluation rubric.
Interview guide.
Reference check questionnaire.
Hiring tracker.
Role-specific checklists for hiring lead, interview coordinator, and reference checker.

The Bookkeeper hiring process document adapts this pattern for a financial role with stronger confidentiality, accuracy, and control expectations.

Section: Evaluation And Accountability Frameworks.

The prior evaluation documents use a healthy tone: clarity, encouragement, accountability, support, and growth rather than punishment.

That tone should be kept if KBC creates a future Bookkeeper evaluation framework.

Section: Patterns To Improve.

Section: Do Not Store Sensitive Hiring Records In This Repository.

The older repository includes completed candidate applications and completed reference-check documents in working folders. Those should not be copied into this financial operations repository.

This repository should not store:

Actual applications.
Reference-check notes.
Background-check results.
Payroll documents.
Social Security numbers.
Bank account numbers.
Donor records.
Confidential personnel details.

Section: Replace One-Time Google Docs Conversion With Repeatable Export Workflow.

The old convert_gdocs.py script was useful for a one-time conversion from Google Docs shortcuts to DOCX files. It should not be the main workflow going forward.

This repository should prefer:

Markdown source files.
Repeatable export scripts.
GitHub Actions export artifacts.
Dated release bundles.
Clear validation checks.

Section: Avoid PDF-Only As The Only Final Source.

The old final-approved folder is useful for sharing, but PDF-only final storage makes future updates harder.

Recommended pattern:

Keep approved Markdown source.
Generate approved PDF/DOCX as release copies.
Store dated release bundles.
Preserve superseded versions in archive/ when needed.

Section: Normalize Encoding And Formatting.

Some older converted Markdown files show encoding artifacts around dashes, arrows, and checkboxes. New Markdown should use plain ASCII where practical and avoid fragile special characters in templates.

Section: Make Workflow Easier For Non-Developers.

The current Bash export workflow works well in GitHub Actions, but local Windows export is still too technical.

Future improvement:

Add a Windows-friendly scripts/export.ps1.
Add a simple scripts/validate-exports.ps1, if practical.
Keep GitHub Actions as the no-install option for non-developers.

Section: Recommended Next Improvements.

Create a Bookkeeper evaluation framework after the job description and supervision path are settled.
Create a formal Bookkeeper onboarding checklist after software and financial procedures are confirmed.
Create a Windows-friendly export script for local use.
Decide whether final approved release files should use KBC formal naming convention.
Keep candidate-specific hiring materials outside this repository and store them only in the approved confidential Personnel Committee location.

Section: Current Changes Made From This Review.

Updated docs/roles/bookkeeper-financial-administrator-job-description.md to better match KBC job-description structure.
Updated templates/job-description-template.md to reflect the stronger KBC format.
Added docs/roles/bookkeeper-hiring-process.md based on the prior Custodian hiring process, adapted for a financial role.

Needs Committee Review: Personnel Committee should confirm whether this document workflow direction fits current church practice.

End of chapter: Document Workflow Notes.

End of listening script.

Before making decisions, verify the latest source documents, committee minutes, church bylaws, and any needed professional guidance.
